questions.question: What does line coverage directly measure?
questions.explanation: Line coverage reports which lines executed; it does not validate semantic correctness by itself.

questions.question: What is branch coverage checking that line coverage is not?
options: That every conditional true/false path is exercised
questions.explanation: Branch coverage tracks whether both outcomes of each decision point were taken, which line coverage can miss entirely.

questions.question: Why can 100% line coverage still miss defects?
options: Because assertions may be weak and branch semantics under-tested
questions.explanation: Execution without strong claims can miss wrong behavior even when all lines run.

questions.question: Best use of coverage in practice?
options: Map untested regions and combine with deeper checks
questions.explanation: Coverage is best as a discovery signal, paired with stronger techniques for confidence.

questions.question: What does mutation testing reveal that coverage cannot?
options: Whether tests actually detect injected faults
How fast the test suite runs
questions.explanation: Mutation testing introduces small code changes and checks whether existing tests fail, measuring test strength rather than mere execution.

_meta.title: Coverage - What It Tells You and What It Doesn't
